key_points: Move-in logistics at Garland parking garage caused heat stroke incidents
Campus has expanded dramatically — new residential complexes where tennis courts once stood
Older dorms like Reid Hall historically had no AC on upper floors
key_quotes: Parking staff closing in 5 min wouldn't let us drop stuff off in the lot by the room... Everyone helping got sick and almost had heat stroke from the Garland parking garage
Freshman year, I was stuck in Reid Hall on the 8th floor without AC
Now there's a huge residential complex next to it. This area used to be the tennis courts

key_points: Greek life dominates — harder to make friends if not in a fraternity or sorority
SEC football culture is central to campus social identity
Party scene is real; international and exchange students specifically seek it out
key_quotes: I'm not sure I'd fully fit in there, and I've heard it can be harder to make friends if you're not involved in Greek life
I don't really care about the academics, but i'd really like to go to a university with a fun and engaging campus, and party culture
Both of my older brothers go to UChicago... I do want a typical college experience, but I won't be joining a sorority wherever I go

key_points: No specific on-campus dining mentions — a notable data gap
Fayetteville city food scene gets indirect positive mentions
Little Rock's food scene praised, but that's 2+ hours from campus
key_quotes: I've lived here for a few years now and definitely agree with everything you said about the food and people
Best damn Cheeseburger, and yes, till 5 a.m.

key_points: No student mentions of counseling services or mental health support on campus
Pre-med students warned about burnout risk at peer schools — implied pressure exists
LGBTQ students with unsupportive families flagged UArk as a concern for social safety
key_quotes: We've already had discourse about my lgbtq identity, and it's likely they would stop paying me if I don't behave within their Christian, heterosexual standards
i wouldn't apply to UM, it's hella expensive and they're pre med is notoriously difficult and most pre med kids burn out with their party lifestyle

key_points: Fellows Program offers full ride including housing, research, and study abroad — a standout deal
National Merit Finalists get $40,000 Chancellor's Merit Scholarship over four years
In-state tuition is genuinely affordable; OOS students should negotiate hard
key_quotes: I've been accepted into the Fellows program at my state school (University of Arkansas) and would graduate without having to pay anything for tuition, housing, research, study abroad, etc.
The U of A awards the Chancellor's Merit Scholarship, valued at $40,000 over four years, to all National Merit finalists
Arkansas would be approximately $15,000 a year

key_points: Business and Walton College consistently praised; Walmart proximity creates real-world ties
Engineering described as 'very competitive and challenging' with strong career fairs
Honors College is selective enough that Vanderbilt parents confused acceptance rates
key_quotes: Arkansas has the better business program. Courtesy of Walmart
I'm currently studying Civil Engineering at the university of Arkansas. It's a very competitive and challenging program, but you learn a lot and have giant career fairs and lots of clubs to network
CEO of the top Fortune 100 company went to the University of Arkansas

key_points: Parking enforcement is rigid and unhelpful during high-stress move-in situations
Admissions office responsive enough for international students to reach out directly
Online Eversity division dissolved into University of Grantham — credits in limbo for some students
key_quotes: Parking staff closing in 5 min wouldn't let us drop stuff off in the lot by the room and get a cart... it took hours and people got heat stroke
During an extended break, Eversity changed/sold to the University of Grantham... I was informed that all of my credit would transfer but that I would have to complete a minimum of 30 hours

key_points: Fayetteville is consistently described as a great college town with strong growth
Campus has grown massively — alumni from the 1980s barely recognize it today
Northwest Arkansas tech and retail hub (Walmart HQ nearby) adds real career proximity
key_quotes: I have a few friends in the Honors College too and I live by Fayetteville! It's a good area of Arkansas
I have heard that Fayetteville is becoming Austin 2.0, would locals agree with this?
Oh my, can't believe how much this place has grown!

key_points: Walton College business grads benefit directly from Walmart and Walmart-adjacent companies
Engineering students get strong internship pipeline and networking through career fairs
Fortune 100 CEO (Walmart's) attended UArk — signal for business-track students
key_quotes: CEO of the top Fortune 100 company went to the University of Arkansas
If you want to major in business (Walmart) or pre-med fields, take a harder look at Arkansas
I'm currently studying Civil Engineering at the university of Arkansas... have giant career fairs and lots of clubs to network and meet with companies

key_points: Full-ride Fellows Program is one of the best value deals of any state school nationally
In-state cost (~$15-22k/year all-in) makes it a legitimate choice over pricier OOS schools
Reddit consistently tells students: take the UArk money over $83k/year dream schools
key_quotes: GO TO ARKANSAS. That's a terrific Fellows program. YOU WILL HAVE SO MANY WONDERFUL OPPORTUNITIES... Plus it's free.
$83k a year is INSANE. No school is worth that much (outside of HYPSM).
Arkansas would be approximately $15,000 a year

key_points: Strong SEC sports culture — football and basketball are genuine social anchors
Campus feels welcoming for in-state students; OOS and POC students express more uncertainty
Alumni sentiment is warm and nostalgic; international students are drawn by reputation and culture
key_quotes: I left UofA in 2016... UofA has never left me
I was inspired by one of your notable alumni, featured in a movie, Brandon Burlsworth
Woo Pig Sooie. Yes, you made the right call

key_points: **Social inclusion**: Greek life gatekeeps friendships; non-members report social isolation
**LGBTQ climate**: conservative Christian culture noted as a real concern for queer students
**Diversity**: multiple POC applicants flagged low minority representation as a serious worry

Walmart's HQ proximity creates unique supply chain, retail, and finance internship pipelines
Honors College is selective enough to surprise people — rivals some private school programs
